data.description:
## Finding (pre-beta deprecation sweep — meta-finding)
The `config.py:429-458` deprecation-removal registry asserts completeness ("Any alias added later MUST get a row here") but is scoped only to env/cfg/lib aliases and MISSES several genuinely-deprecated, mostly warning-emitting, user-facing surfaces. Two hygiene problems compound it: some scheduled removals emit NO runtime signal (users learn only when deleted), and the six "permanent, never-remove" ergonomic env renames nonetheless log "…is deprecated…" on every read.

Citations (registry gaps):
- MCP `list_epics` tool `src/rebar/_mcp_reads.py:239-252` (suppresses its own DeprecationWarning at :247).
- env `REBAR_LLM_MAX_ITERS` `src/rebar/llm/config.py:15,363-371` (warns via logger; lives in the LLM config layer, outside the core registry).
- CLI `--verdict-hash` `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:45` (accepted-but-ignored; warns on stderr).
- cfg value `reconciler.lock_backend="file"` `src/rebar/_config_schema.py:335-336` (deprecated + ignored).
- Registry table `src/rebar/config.py:444-458`.
- Silent scheduled removals: `edit_ticket(tags=)` `_commands/composer.py:345-358` (no warning), flat `.rebar/config.conf` reader `config.py:303,380`, CLI `--no-sync` alias `_engine_support/reads_cli.py:624`.
- Contradiction: permanent renames warn "deprecated" `config.py:461-477`.

## Remediation
Make the registry actually complete and the signalling consistent (pre-beta hygiene; no removal here):
1. Add rows for the 4 uncatalogued deprecated surfaces (MCP list_epics, REBAR_LLM_MAX_ITERS, --verdict-hash, lock_backend="file") with horizons.
2. Ensure every scheduled-for-removal surface emits a DeprecationWarning THIS release (so beta users get a signal before v1.0.0): edit_ticket(tags=), the MCP list_epics tool (stop suppressing), the flat-conf reader, --no-sync.
3. Reconcile the "permanent rename yet warns deprecated" contradiction: either soften the wording for the six permanent env renames, or schedule them — pick one before beta.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All deprecated user-facing surfaces have a registry row with a horizon (registry is genuinely complete)
- [ ] Every scheduled-removal surface emits a DeprecationWarning in this release (no silent removals at v1.0.0)
- [ ] The permanent-rename env aliases' log wording no longer claims "deprecated" (or they are scheduled) — consistent messaging
- [ ] A test/CI check asserts new aliases get a registry row
data.title: Close the deprecation-registry completeness gap + fix inconsistent deprecation signalling
